w:r.w:t: Google Maps Lead Intelligence & Competitor Analysis Platform
LeadMap Pro — B2B Lead Intelligence SaaS

LeadMap Pro is a cloud-based SaaS web application that enables businesses, sales teams, marketing agencies, and entrepreneurs to discover, qualify, and export high-intent B2B leads by intelligently querying real-world data from Google Maps and supplementary public web sources.

Unlike existing tools that are either too technical (raw scrapers) or too limited (browser extensions), LeadMap Pro is designed to be the definitive no-code lead intelligence platform — combining Google Maps data extraction, AI-powered enrichment, competitor mapping, and CRM-ready export into a single, beautiful, real-time dashboard.

"To be the world's most intuitive lead intelligence platform — turning Google Maps' 200 million+ business listings into a real-time, filterable, AI-enriched lead database that any business can use in under 60 seconds, with no technical knowledge required."
The core problem this product solves is threefold:
Finding qualified leads is manual and time-consuming. Sales teams spend hours copying business data from Google Maps row by row.
Existing scraping tools are either too technical (requiring code and proxy management) or too limited (capped at 120 results, no enrichment).
There is no single tool that combines lead discovery, competitor mapping, review intelligence, and CRM export in one clean interface.

The web scraping industry crossed $1.03 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $2 billion by 2030 (14.2% YoY growth — Mordor Intelligence). Google Maps contains 200M+ business listings across 220+ countries, with 2 billion monthly active users. 88% of consumers use Google Maps to find local businesses (BrightLocal, 2026). The Google Places API costs $17–$35 per 1,000 requests for bulk data — making a dedicated SaaS solution 10x more cost-effective at scale.
Deliver a no-code lead generation tool accessible to non-technical users within 60 seconds of signup.
Extract and enrich real-time Google Maps business data at scale, bypassing the 120-result cap.
Provide AI-powered competitor analysis, gap detection, and market saturation scoring.
Enable CRM-ready data export (CSV, Excel, JSON, Webhook, Zapier/Make).
Build a legally compliant, rate-limited scraping engine using a hybrid API + scraping architecture.
Launch a subscription SaaS model with a meaningful free tier and tiered paid plans.
Industry and niche selector with curated taxonomy (100+ categories)
Geographic targeting: country, state/region, city, postal code, custom radius
Real-time Google Maps data extraction: name, address, phone, website, rating, review count, hours, category
AI-powered website enrichment: email, social profiles, tech stack detection
Competitor analysis panel: density map, rating comparison, review velocity
Export: CSV, Excel, JSON, clipboard copy
User authentication, subscription billing, usage metering
Out of scope for V1.0 (planned V2+):
AI review sentiment analysis at scale — V2
Google My Business — the platform behind Google Maps business listings
Google's official Places API for accessing map business data
A qualifying business contact extracted from Google Maps with enriched contact data
The process of visiting a business website to extract additional data (email, social links, tech stack)
A specific sub-category of an industry (e.g. 'Wedding Photography' under 'Photography')
The server-side headless browser pipeline that extracts data from Google Maps
A technique to bypass the 60–120 result cap by dividing a target area into smaller geographic cells
An algorithmic quality rating (0–100) assigned to each extracted lead
LeadMap Pro serves multiple user segments, each with distinct needs and willingness to pay:
Build call lists for a specific city + niche
Competitor analysis + local SEO audits for clients
Find local competitors and understand market positioning
Territory mapping and ICP qualification at volume
Identify hiring companies in specific industries and areas
Map commercial landlords, businesses vacating space
Bulk data collection for industry and geographic analysis
The following tools represent the current competitive landscape. LeadMap Pro is differentiated by combining all capabilities into a single, no-code, real-time interface with AI enrichment.
No competitor mapping or niche taxonomy
Complex UI, not purpose-built for Maps
LeadMap Pro is positioned as the only tool that delivers all five pillars simultaneously:
Search, filter, and preview leads in a live map + table view — no CSV download loop
Bypass the 120-result cap by auto-dividing areas into a geographic grid of sub-searches
After Maps extraction, automatically crawl business websites for emails, social links, and tech stack
Visualize competitor density, rating distribution, review velocity, and market gaps on a heatmap
Automatically score every lead (0–100) based on recency, completeness, rating, and review activity
Scraping publicly available Google Maps data is a legally nuanced area. The following framework governs LeadMap Pro's approach:

In hiQ Labs v. LinkedIn (2019, affirmed 2022), the US Ninth Circuit ruled that scraping publicly visible data (data accessible without logging in) does not violate the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act (CFAA). This ruling is the primary legal basis for commercially operating Google Maps scraping tools. Google's Terms of Service prohibit scraping, but ToS violations are a private contract matter (risk: account ban), not a criminal or civil law matter.
Only scrape public, non-authenticated data. No account creation. No bypassing of access controls.
B2B business data (names, addresses, phones) is not personal data under GDPR. Individual emails trigger Article 6(1)(f) legitimate interest. Include opt-out in all enriched contact lists.
Provide data deletion mechanism. Do not sell individual personal data. Honor opt-out requests within 15 business days.
All exported lead lists include unsubscribe guidance. Platform provides CAN-SPAM compliant export templates.
Same as EU GDPR. ICO registration required if processing UK resident data at scale.
Do not use Google API keys for scraping beyond free tier. Rate-limit all requests. Do not store Google Maps data beyond 30 days for API-derived data (scraping has no caching restriction).
All extracted business data is treated as commercial/factual data, not personal data, under the GDPR distinction.
Individual email addresses collected via website enrichment are tagged as 'enriched contact' and subject to opt-out rights.
Scraped data is stored in encrypted databases with a configurable data retention period (default: 90 days).
No scraped data is sold to third parties. Data belongs to the user who initiated the scrape.
All scraping operations are rate-limited server-side to avoid causing server harm to Google's infrastructure.
Users must agree to a Data Use Policy before exporting lead lists, confirming compliant outreach use.

LeadMap Pro uses a decoupled, microservices-inspired architecture with three distinct layers: the client-facing Next.js web application, the Node.js API gateway, and the Python-based distributed scraping engine.
API GATEWAY (Node.js / Express + Auth + Rate Limiting)
SCRAPING ENGINE ENRICHMENT ENGINE AI SCORING ENGINE
JOB QUEUE (Redis + BullMQ) ←→ PROXY POOL (Residential IPs)
SSR for SEO, App Router for streaming UI, TypeScript for type safety
Interactive heatmaps, cluster markers, polygon drawing for area selection
High-concurrency async API, WebSocket support for streaming results
JWT-based auth, social login (Google, GitHub), magic links
Full JS rendering needed for dynamic Google Maps content
Bright Data or Oxylabs residential proxies
Residential IPs to avoid detection; auto-rotation per request
Fast HTML parsing for business websites to extract emails and social links
OpenAI GPT-4o API + custom ML model
Lead scoring, niche classification, review sentiment summary
Async job management for scrape tasks, retries, priorities
Relational storage for users, leads, campaigns, search history
Rate limit tracking, session cache, frequently searched data
Subscription management, usage-based metering, webhook handling
Error tracking, performance monitoring, user analytics

Google Maps only displays a maximum of 60–120 business listings per search query, regardless of how many businesses exist in an area. This is the primary technical challenge all Google Maps tools must solve.

LeadMap Pro's Grid Search Engine divides any target geographic area into a dynamic grid of smaller sub-areas (cells). Each cell is small enough that it contains fewer than 60 businesses, meaning a full extraction is possible from each cell. The cells are then merged, deduplicated by Place ID, and returned as a unified result set.

Algorithm: (1) User defines target area. (2) System queries the area with an initial broad search. (3) If result count approaches 60, the area is recursively split into 4 quadrants. (4) Each quadrant is queried independently. (5) Results are merged and deduplicated using Google Place IDs as unique keys.
Residential Proxy Rotation: Every request is routed through a different residential IP address via Bright Data or Oxylabs pools. Residential IPs mimic real user traffic.
Request Rate Limiting: Server enforces minimum 2–5 second delays between requests per proxy, with randomized jitter. Configurable per user tier.
Browser Fingerprint Randomization: Playwright launches with randomized viewport, user agent, language headers, and timezone to avoid fingerprint-based detection.
Session Rotation: New browser contexts are created per scrape job, not per request, to mimic natural user sessions.
CAPTCHA Handling: Integration with 2Captcha or Anti-Captcha API for automated CAPTCHA solving when encountered.
Retry Logic: Exponential backoff with a maximum of 3 retries per failed request before job failure notification.
After extracting base Google Maps data, the enrichment pipeline runs asynchronously for each lead with a website URL:
Website crawler visits the business website using Scrapy with a rotating user agent.
Email Extraction: Parses the homepage, /contact, /about pages for email addresses using regex + NLP.
Social Profile Detection: Identifies LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X links in page HTML.
Tech Stack Fingerprinting: Reads HTTP headers, HTML meta tags, and script sources to identify CMS, analytics, and advertising pixels (e.g. WordPress, Shopify, Google Ads pixel).
Contact Page Crawl: If no email found on homepage, visits the /contact or /contact-us path specifically.
All enrichment data is stored asynchronously and pushed to the user's results panel via WebSocket in real time.
Users can register via email/password, Google OAuth, or magic link. Email verification required before first search.
New users complete a 3-step onboarding: (1) Select their primary industry, (2) Enter their target region, (3) Run their first sample search with 10 free results.
Authenticated users see a home dashboard showing: search history, saved lead lists, usage meter (credits used / credits remaining), and recent export activity.

A hierarchical category picker with 12 top-level industries and 100+ niche sub-categories. Users can also type a custom keyword. Examples: [Healthcare > Dentists], [Food & Beverage > Coffee Shops], [Trades > Plumbers].

Users can define their search area via: (1) City/town name, (2) Postal/ZIP code, (3) State or region, (4) Country-wide, (5) Custom radius in km/miles from a point, (6) Drawn polygon on an interactive map.
Before running a search, users can set: minimum star rating (e.g. 4.0+), minimum review count, has website (yes/no), is claimed listing (yes/no), open now filter, keyword in business name.
Results appear in the UI progressively as the scraping engine extracts them — not as a single bulk result after completion. Users see a live counter and can preview data before the job finishes.

When a search area contains more than 60 potential results, the system automatically activates Grid Search mode, splitting the area into sub-cells and merging results with de-duplication. The user sees this visually on the map.
After results load, users can filter the results table by: rating range, review count, has email, has website, has social profiles, lead score range, tech stack detected.
Results displayed in a sortable, paginated table with columns: Business Name, Category, Rating, Reviews, Address, Phone, Website, Email (enriched), Lead Score, Actions.
Clicking any lead opens a side panel showing: full address, all phone numbers, website, social links, operating hours, Google Maps embed, review summary, tech stack, and a notes field.

Every lead receives an automatic score (0–100) based on: data completeness (30pts), Google rating (20pts), review count (20pts), website present (15pts), email found (15pts). Score is displayed as a colored badge.
Users can create named lead lists (e.g. 'Q2 Miami Dentists') and save individual leads or entire search results to a list. Lists persist across sessions.
Users can add private notes and custom tags (e.g. 'Contacted', 'Hot Lead', 'Not Qualified') to individual leads. Tags are filterable across lists.
Parallel to the results table, a map view shows all extracted businesses as clustered pins. Clicking a pin shows a quick preview card with name, rating, and lead score.
A toggle switches the map to heatmap mode, showing competitor concentration by geographic area. Darker areas = more competitors. Lighter areas = market opportunity gaps.
AI analyzes the geographic distribution of businesses and highlights 'under-served zones' — areas with high population proximity but low business density for the selected niche.
For any search, a dedicated 'Competitors' tab shows: top 10 businesses by review count, average rating for the area, rating distribution chart, review velocity (new reviews per month estimate).

Selecting a competitor shows: all their reviews with sentiment summary (AI-generated), their website tech stack, their social presence, estimated monthly website traffic tier (Low/Medium/High), and a SWOT-style summary.
Users can generate a one-page 'Market Benchmark' PDF showing: average rating in the niche, review count percentiles, top keywords in reviews, and where a specific business ranks.
Users can export any lead list or search result as a clean CSV with all available fields. Column visibility is configurable before export.
Multi-sheet Excel export with a 'Summary' sheet (search metadata) and a 'Leads' sheet (full data). Includes conditional formatting for lead scores.
Pro and Business tier users receive an API key to query their saved lead lists programmatically via a REST endpoint. Supports pagination and field selection.
When a scrape job completes, LeadMap Pro can POST results to a configured webhook URL. Compatible with Zapier, Make (Integromat), and n8n for CRM automation.
One-click copy of any business's phone, email, website, or full address to clipboard.
< 10 seconds for CSV, < 20 seconds for Excel
Scraping workers are horizontally scalable using containerized Python workers deployed on Railway, Fly.io, or AWS ECS.
Job queue (BullMQ + Redis) decouples user-facing API from scraping load — queue backs up gracefully under load.
Database uses connection pooling (PgBouncer) to handle concurrent user requests efficiently.
Frontend is statically generated where possible (Next.js) and served via global CDN (Vercel Edge Network).
Target: Support 10,000 monthly active users and 2 million leads processed per month in Year 1.
All data in transit encrypted with TLS 1.3. All data at rest encrypted with AES-256.
API authentication via JWT with 24-hour token expiry and refresh token rotation.
Rate limiting on all API endpoints: 100 requests/minute for authenticated users, 10/minute for unauthenticated.
SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries (Prisma ORM).
XSS prevention via React's built-in escaping and Content Security Policy headers.
CSRF protection via SameSite cookie policy and CSRF tokens on state-changing requests.
All user passwords hashed with bcrypt (cost factor 12). Not stored in plaintext.
Scraping API keys and proxy credentials stored in environment variables / secrets manager. Never in source code.
WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance for all core UI components.
Keyboard navigable search and results interface.
Time-to-first-lead target: a new user should be able to complete their first search in under 60 seconds with no documentation.
Responsive design: fully functional on desktop (primary), tablet (secondary). Mobile-readable but not mobile-optimized in V1.
All loading states, empty states, and error states must have clear, actionable messaging.
id, email, plan_tier, credits_remaining, created_at, stripe_customer_id
id, user_id, query, location, filters, status, created_at, completed_at
A scrape job initiated by a user
id, search_job_id, google_place_id, name, address, phone, website, rating, review_count, category, lat, lng, hours, is_claimed
lead_id, email, social_links (JSON), tech_stack (JSON), enrichment_status, enriched_at
id, user_id, name, description, created_at, lead_count
list_id, lead_id, notes, tags (array), added_at, status
id, user_id, list_id, format, file_url, created_at, download_count
id, user_id, plan, credits_per_month, stripe_subscription_id, current_period_end
Each Lead progresses through the following states:
QUEUED → SCRAPING → SCRAPED → ENRICHING → ENRICHED → SCORED
Each state transition is logged with a timestamp. Users see real-time status via WebSocket push. Failed enrichments still show as valid leads with base Maps data.
As a sales rep, I want to search for all dental clinics in Miami with 4+ stars so I can build a call list for this week.
Search returns all matching clinics with phone numbers. I can filter by rating. I can export to CSV in one click.
As a marketing agency, I want to see a heatmap of my client's competitors across Chicago so I can identify under-served neighborhoods to target.
Map view shows competitor density heatmap. Clicking any area shows business count. Under-served zones are highlighted.
As a SaaS founder, I want to find all restaurants in New York City that are using Square (tech stack) and have a website, so I can pitch them my payments integration.
Tech stack filter is available post-enrichment. I can filter by 'Square detected = true' and export emails only.
As a small business owner, I want to see how my plumbing company compares to competitors in my city in terms of ratings and review count.
Competitor panel shows average rating, review count distribution, and my position relative to competitors.
As a researcher, I want to extract 5,000 coffee shops across the state of California and download the full dataset with coordinates for a mapping study.
Grid search handles state-level area. Full export with lat/lng fields. JSON export available for researchers.
As a recruiter, I want to find all staffing agencies in Dallas with a website so I can reach out about a partnership.
'has website = true' filter available. Email enrichment runs automatically. I can export with emails.
1 credit = 1 extracted lead from Google Maps (base data). Enrichment does not cost additional credits in V1.
Credits are consumed when a lead is successfully extracted and returned to the user's results panel.
If a search returns 0 results due to a scraping error, no credits are charged.
Credits roll over for 30 days from the billing date (not calendar month) to avoid end-of-month rush.
Users can purchase top-up credit packs at $5 per 500 credits without upgrading their plan.
Project setup, monorepo, CI/CD pipeline, database schema, auth, Stripe integration, design system
Scraping engine (Playwright + proxy), basic Grid Search, results table, map view, CSV export, Free + Starter plans
Email + social enrichment pipeline, tech stack detection, lead scoring algorithm, WebSocket streaming, Excel export
Competitor panel, density heatmap, market gap detection, saved lists, tags/notes, API access for Pro tier
Webhook/Zapier integration, Benchmark PDF report, referral system, usage analytics dashboard, performance optimisation
Agency/team accounts, CRM integrations (HubSpot, Pipedrive), white-label option, mobile-responsive polish, Series A readiness
Abstract all CSS selectors into a config file. Monitor scraping success rate. Implement fallback XPath selectors. Consider Apify actor as backup data source.
IP blocks / rate limiting by Google
Mandatory residential proxy rotation (Bright Data). Per-worker rate limiting. CAPTCHA service integration. Do not exceed 1 request per 3 seconds per proxy.
Places API is only used as a fallback (not primary). All primary extraction via scraper. Places API usage metered and alerted at $50/month threshold.

Only store business data, not personal data. Individual emails stored with explicit opt-out mechanism. Legal review before EU launch. GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) template for B2B users.
Use Apify's maintained Google Maps actor as a hybrid fallback. Allocate 20% of dev time for scraper maintenance in ongoing sprints.
Scraping workers run on spot/preemptible instances to reduce cost. Redis queue prevents cascading failures. Load test at 10x expected volume before launch.
All API requests must include an Authorization header: Authorization: Bearer <api_key>. API keys are generated per user from the dashboard and can be rotated.
Initiate a new scrape job. Body: { query, location, filters, limit }. Returns: { job_id, estimated_duration }
Poll a scrape job status. Returns: { status, progress, leads_found, leads_enriched }
Retrieve paginated results for a completed job. Params: page, limit, sort_by, filters
Get full detail for a single lead including enrichment data.
List all saved lead lists for the authenticated user.
Create a new lead list. Body: { name, description }
Add lead(s) to a list. Body: { lead_ids: [] }
Generate and return a download URL for CSV/Excel/JSON export of a list. Params: format
Return current credit usage, plan tier, and billing period data.
Register a webhook URL for job completion events. Body: { url, events: [] }
The following 12 industry categories each have 8–12 sub-niches in the full taxonomy (100+ total niches):
Dentists, Physiotherapists, Chiropractors, GP Clinics, Optometrists, Veterinarians, Pharmacies
Restaurants, Coffee Shops, Bakeries, Food Trucks, Catering Companies, Bars, Fast Food
Plumbers, Electricians, HVAC, Roofers, Landscapers, Cleaners, Painters, General Contractors
Clothing Stores, Electronics, Furniture, Bookstores, Pet Shops, Florists, Hardware
Law Firms, Accountants, Financial Advisors, Mortgage Brokers, Insurance Agents, Tax Services
Hair Salons, Nail Salons, Spas, Gyms, Yoga Studios, Personal Trainers, Barbers
Private Schools, Tutors, Music Schools, Driving Schools, Language Schools, Daycares
Estate Agents, Property Management, Commercial Realtors, Storage Facilities
Car Dealerships, Auto Repair, MOT Centers, Car Wash, Tyre Shops, Body Shops
IT Support, Web Agencies, App Developers, CCTV Installation, Computer Repair
Hotels, Event Venues, Wedding Photographers, DJs, Catering, Party Planners
Marketing Agencies, PR Firms, HR Consultants, Management Consultants, Architects
HiQ Labs v. LinkedIn, 938 F.3d 985 (9th Cir. 2019) — Legal basis for public data scraping
Mordor Intelligence: Web Scraping Market Report, 2025 — Industry size $1.03B, projected $2B by 2030
BrightLocal Consumer Review Survey, 2026 — 88% of consumers use Google Maps for local business discovery
Google Maps Platform Pricing — $17–$35 per 1,000 requests (Places API, 2026)
GDPR Article 6(1)(f) — Legitimate interest basis for B2B data processing
CAN-SPAM Act (15 U.S.C. 7701) — US email marketing compliance requirements
Outscraper Pricing Benchmark, March 2026 — $30 per 10,000 leads vs $300–700 via Places API
LeadMap Pro SRS v1.0 | April 2026 | Confidential
